title: Flaky Flatbread Naan
description: A traditional Indian flatbread, made with flaky layers and a crispy crust, served with a variety of accompaniments.
instructions: 1. In a large mixing bowl, combine flaky flour, rye flour, and salt. Mix well.
2. Gradually add water to the flour mixture and knead for 5-7 minutes until a smooth dough forms.
3. Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 30 minutes.
4. Preheat a non-stick skillet or tava over medium heat.
5. Divide the dough into 6-8 equal portions and shape each into a ball.
6. Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 6-7 inches in diameter.
7. Brush the surface of the flatbread with ghee or oil.
8. Place the flatbread in the skillet and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side, until it's puffed and golden brown.
9. Brush the flatbread with norwegian yogurt and sprinkle with showy herbs (if using).
10. Serve hot with your favorite accompaniments.
